hero.label: About Us
hero.heading: Frontier Climate Technologies
hero.description: AI, Carbon Dioxide Removal, and Solar Radiation Management; explored through an African lens.

srm.title: Solar Radiation Management
srm.description: Solar Radiation Management refers to proposed technologies that would reflect a small amount of sunlight back into space to reduce global warming. These technologies are highly contested and carry significant risks and uncertainties—particularly for African climate systems, agriculture, and water resources.

features.modeling: Regional climate modeling for SRM impacts
features.risk: Risk assessment for African agriculture and water systems

ai.title: Artificial Intelligence for Climate Adaptation
ai.description: The absence of Africa-centered governance frameworks and reliance on foreign data centres risks perpetuating inequities in global climate decision-making, delaying real-time AI-supported responses. ECF advocates for local AI infrastructure and expertise to ensure context-specific climate responses that serve African communities first.

features.data: Developing AI for high-quality climate data in water-scarce regions
features.centres: Building local data centres and processing capacity
features.warning: Early warning systems for agriculture, water, and disaster response
features.governance: Ethical AI governance frameworks for climate action

cdr.title: Carbon Dioxide Removal
cdr.description: CDR encompasses both nature-based solutions and technological approaches to removing carbon dioxide from the atmosphere. Africa's diverse ecosystems—from forests to coastlines—offer significant potential for CDR, but solutions must be tailored to local contexts.

features.natureBased: Nature-based CDR solutions (biochar, afforestation, soil carbon)
features.accounting: Carbon accounting and MRV (Monitoring, Reporting, Verification)
features.entrepreneurship: Local CDR entrepreneurship and job creation
